combat_history.event: HMAS Anzac conducted maritime interception operations in the Persian Gulf, boarding suspicious vessels and enforcing UN sanctions
combat_history.significance: First major combat deployment, demonstrated platform's boarding and inspection capabilities

combat_history.event: HMAS Parramatta enforced no-fly zone off Libya, conducted surveillance and humanitarian evacuation support
combat_history.significance: Showed platform's ability to operate in contested airspace with limited air defense capability

combat_history.event: Multiple Anzac-class vessels conducted maritime security operations in Persian Gulf, protecting coalition naval forces from Iranian small boat threats
combat_history.significance: Validated 25mm Typhoon mounts against swarm boat tactics

combat_history.event: HMAS Toowoomba and other Anzac vessels conducted freedom of navigation operations in South China Sea, tracked Chinese naval movements
combat_history.significance: Demonstrated persistent presence capability but highlighted vulnerability to advanced anti-ship missiles

combat_history.event: HMAS Sydney (planned deployment) to Red Sea following Houthi attacks on commercial shipping, equipped with upgraded ESSM Block 2 missiles
combat_history.significance: First operational test of ASMD-upgraded platforms against ballistic missile threats

modernization.description: Complete combat system upgrade including CEAFAR2-L AESA radar, 32-cell Mk 41 VLS, ESSM Block 2 missiles, and Saab 9LV Mk 3E combat management system. RAN vessels only.
modernization.impact: Transformed from patrol frigates to credible area air defense platforms, quadrupled engagement envelope

modernization.description: Integration of Kongsberg NSM anti-ship missiles using existing VLS cells or deck-mounted launchers, providing over-the-horizon strike capability
modernization.impact: Would provide first credible anti-ship capability beyond helicopter-launched weapons

modernization.description: Planned upgrade for New Zealand vessels including new radar, combat management system, and possibly limited VLS capability
modernization.impact: Would bring RNZN vessels closer to RAN ASMD standard but budget constraints may limit scope

vulnerabilities.description: Despite ASMD upgrades, limited magazine depth (32 VLS cells) and lack of long-range interceptors makes them vulnerable to saturation attacks from advanced anti-ship missiles like YJ-18 or Zircon
vulnerabilities.context: In high-intensity conflict with peer adversaries, could be overwhelmed by multiple missile salvos
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Rely on layered defense with other platforms, standoff tactics, and superior situational awareness from CEAFAR radar

vulnerabilities.description: Limited hardening against directed energy weapons and high-power microwave attacks, aging electronic systems vulnerable to jamming
vulnerabilities.context: China and Russia developing advanced EW capabilities specifically targeting Western naval platforms
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Ongoing upgrades to electronic warfare suites, but fundamental platform limitations remain

vulnerabilities.description: Small crews (163 personnel) face significant workload during extended Pacific deployments, limited redundancy in key positions
vulnerabilities.context: Australia's geographic isolation requires 3-4 month deployments with minimal port calls
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Automation improvements in ASMD upgrade help, but fundamental crew size constraint remains

vulnerabilities.description: ESSM Block 2 and Phalanx CIWS inadequate against hypersonic glide vehicles or high-speed ballistic missiles
vulnerabilities.context: China deploying DF-21D and DF-26 anti-ship ballistic missiles, Russia developing Zircon hypersonic missiles
vulnerabilities.mitigation: No current solution, requires cooperation with higher-tier air defense platforms

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Regional presence operations, ASW patrols, coalition task group escort, freedom of navigation operations
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Operates independently or with allied frigates/destroyers, supported by AOR replenishment vessel
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: High operational tempo strains maintenance cycles, CEAFAR radar requires specialized shore support

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 054A has superior missile loadout (32 HHQ-16 SAMs + 8 YJ-83 AShMs) and better multi-warfare capability, but Anzac has superior radar and ASW systems post-ASMD upgrade
peer_comparisons.video_angle: David vs Goliath: How Australia's upgraded frigates stack against China's most numerous warship

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Mogami is newer with integrated mast and modular mission systems, but Anzac ASMD has proven AESA radar and deeper missile magazine. Both emphasize ASW in Pacific theater
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Allied frigate comparison: Why Australia and Japan chose different approaches to the same problem

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Russian frigate emphasizes strike warfare with Kalibr missiles and has superior anti-ship capability, but Anzac ASMD has better air defense radar and electronic warfare systems
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Cold War echoes: How Russian and Australian frigate designs reflect different naval philosophies

peer_comparisons.key_differences: FREMM is larger with more VLS cells and multi-mission capability, but Anzac is more cost-effective and optimized for Pacific operations with superior endurance
peer_comparisons.video_angle: European sophistication vs Pacific pragmatism: Comparing frigate design philosophies
